fault_hypothesis: On line 68, `adjusted_now` is computed as `now - _CLOCK_SKEW_TOLERANCE` (subtracting the tolerance), but it should be `now + _CLOCK_SKEW_TOLERANCE` (adding tolerance) so that the expiry check on line 73 (`adjusted_now > expires_at`) correctly rejects entries when `now >= expires_at`. Because the tolerance is subtracted, `adjusted_now` is smaller than `now`, causing entries at or past expiry to be incorrectly returned.

constraints.intent: After computing adjusted_now, it should be >= now (tolerance added), not less than now (tolerance subtracted).
adjusted_now must be at least as large as now so the expiry boundary check is conservative.
Before using adjusted_now in the expiry comparison, it must be >= now to correctly detect boundary expiry.
The cached value is only returned when adjusted_now is strictly less than expires_at; at boundary (now==expires_at) the entry should be expired.
